content: The 2024-2025 SOTA work on scalable active agent memory can be reframed to solve a longstanding flaw in curiosity-driven reinforcement learning (ICM/RND): the tendency to get stuck in unproductive infinite loops of novelty-seeking in noisy, uninformative environments. By borrowing the hierarchical selective retrieval mechanisms from cutting-edge active agent memory systems, curiosity-driven agents can automatically prune low-value, transient novel experiences from their memory buffer and prioritize persistent, learnable novel patterns that drive long-term task progress.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
